spec_match.rationale: The demo now uses the current CSS Text 4 grammar with text-fit: grow and text-fit: shrink instead of obsolete contain/scale-down values; DevTools MCP verification is still blocked in this session.

interactive.rationale: The horizontal resize shell and container width range slider remain wired to the comparison columns, but the required chrome-devtools-mcp click-through verification was unavailable.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and page copy now describe grow, shrink, and fixed-size text rather than non-existent contain/scale-down modes.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers grow and shrink support detection, but it still lacks user-editable text and the fallback path was not exercised through DevTools MCP.

references.rationale: The see also section properly links to the chromestatus.com entry for feature 5104141688635392.

design_system.rationale: The page conforms to the design system, utilizing the specified CSS variables and clean typographic layout.

openQuestions.title: Run DevTools MCP verification for text-fit grow and shrink
openQuestions.detail: chrome-devtools-mcp was not exposed in this repair session. Reopen /v150/css-text-fit-property/text-fit-demo/ in Chrome or Chrome Canary, verify the support note reports current text-fit support in Chrome 151, inspect that the grow and shrink elements expose text-fit: grow and text-fit: shrink in computed styles, exercise the width slider, and check /v150/css-text-fit-property/conformance/.

openQuestions.title: Allow user text input to test length and wrapping edge cases
openQuestions.detail: Currently the demo uses fixed text. Adding a simple text input box so users can type their own strings would help them observe how text-fit handles extreme line lengths, spaces, and potential overflow when the minimum font-size limit is reached.

summary: Static repair replaced obsolete text-fit: contain and text-fit: scale-down syntax with current text-fit: grow and text-fit: shrink syntax, and refreshed the feature conformance suite. Browser verification remains blocked until chrome-devtools-mcp is available.
